Notes & Verdict: Open-source, free, steeper learning curve, requires self-hosting on Pi, poor native multi-printer (requires Docker/multiple instances), excellent community. VERDICT: Best for DIY/learning, not for production farm scaling

Cloud-native, easy UI, strong Etsy integration, AI failure detection, excellent multi-printer support, great for small-medium farms, $10/month for 3-10 printers. VERDICT: Recommended for Phase 1-2 (best value for <10 printers)

Local server + cloud option, strong API, good multi-printer (4+ on single Pi), less intuitive UI than SimplyPrint, strong professional community, $199/year for unlimited printers. VERDICT: Good for self-hosted preference and budget-conscious scaling
Cloud-based, easy onboarding, multiple printer support, less API depth than competitors, lower community activity. VERDICT: OK for basic multi-printer, limited advanced features
Cloud wrapper around OctoPrint, AI sentry for failure detection, mobile app, excellent for tinkerers, limited advanced orchestration. VERDICT: Good for hobbyist farms, not production-grade

Premium SaaS, Filametrics integration, auto job routing, advanced scheduling, expensive, best-in-class for large farms (10+ printers), full business OS. VERDICT: Best for Phase 3+ (8+ printers, mature operation)

Fleet management, batch processing, API-first design, queue optimization, good for medium farms (4-8 printers), less mainstream than SimplyPrint. VERDICT: Solid mid-market option, comparable to Printago but less full-featured

Official Bambu app, cloud-only, no LAN mode, limited multi-printer orchestration, restricted to Bambu Labs printers, simple but constrained. VERDICT: Acceptable for Phase 0-1 single printer, insufficient for farm scaling

API-first, Zapier/Make integration, 8000+ app connectors, inventory tracking, workflow automation, newer entrant, growing community. VERDICT: Good for integration-heavy workflows, Phase 2+ with custom integrations

Open-source, GitHub-based, supports OctoPrint/Moonraker/PrusaLink/Bambu, highly flexible, requires developer skills, active community. VERDICT: For technical teams, strong Docker deployment, Phase 2+ for custom needs
